2605.07271.title: Understanding Performance Collapse in Layer-Pruned Large Language Models via Decision Representation Transitions
2605.07271.paper_summary: Layer pruning efficiently reduces Large Language Model (LLM) computational costs but often triggers sudden performance collapse. Existing representation-based analyses struggle to explain this mechanism. We propose studying pruning through decision representation. Focusing on multiple-choice tasks, we introduce two metrics, Decision Margin and Option Frequency, and an Iterative Pruning method to analyze layer-wise decision dynamics. Our findings reveal a sharp decision transition that partitions the network into two stages: a Silent Phase, where the model cannot yet predict the correct answer, and a Decisive Phase, where the correct prediction emerges. We also find that pruning the Decisive Phase has minimal impact, whereas pruning the Silent Phase triggers immediate performance collapse, highlighting its extreme sensitivity to structural changes. Therefore, we conclude that pruning-induced collapse stems from disrupting the Silent Phase, which prevents the critical decision transition from occurring.
2605.07271.paper_summary_zh: 層修剪有效降低大型語言模型（LLM）的計算成本，但常常會引發突然的性能崩潰。現有的基於表示的分析難以解釋這一機制。我們提議通過決策表示來研究修剪。專注於多選任務，我們引入了兩個指標：決策邊際和選項頻率，以及一種迭代修剪方法來分析層級決策動態。我們的研究結果揭示了一個劇烈的決策轉變，將網絡劃分為兩個階段：一個靜默階段，在這個階段模型尚無法預測正確答案，以及一個決定性階段，在這個階段正確的預測出現。我們還發現，修剪決定性階段的影響最小，而修剪靜默階段則會立即引發性能崩潰，突顯出其對結構變化的極端敏感性。因此，我們得出結論，修剪引起的崩潰源於干擾靜默階段，這阻止了關鍵決策轉變的發生。
2605.07271.author: Boyu Shi et.al.
2605.07271.authors: Boyu Shi, Chang Liu, ChuanBao Gao, Xu Yang, Xin Geng
